title: Change Analyst
description: **Joining Arup** Arup’s purpose, shared values and collaborative approach has set us apart for over 75 years, guiding how we shape a better world. Arup’s Digital Technology group is home to our Service Management Excellence team, a collaborative and forward‑thinking group that drives best practice, service quality and continual improvement across our global technology landscape. We help shape the way Arup designs, delivers and operates its digital services, ensuring they are reliable, well‑governed and continually evolving. As part of this team, you’ll contribute to strengthening our Change Enablement practice, enabling safer, more predictable and more efficient change across our services. **The Opportunity** At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective – in which we encourage individuality to thrive. Our strength comes from how we respect, share and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives and ideas. You will have the opportunity do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, to our members and to the clients and communities we serve. * As a Change Enablement Specialist, you’ll play a central role in coordinating, assessing and supporting the delivery of changes across Arup’s global digital environment. You will manage Change Requests end‑to‑end, ensuring they are well‑assessed, well‑communicated and safely implemented. You will also chair CAB/ECAB meetings, maintain high‑quality change data and collaborate closely with technical teams to ensure changes land effectively and with minimal risk. * This role offers an excellent opportunity to build your expertise in IT service management while contributing to tangible improvements in service stability and digital delivery. What’s unique about this role — what’s in it for you? * You will be the operational owner of the Change Enablement lifecycle, coordinating changes that directly support Arup’s global digital operations. * You will chair CAB and ECAB sessions, developing confidence and capability as a facilitator and trusted advisor. * You will work closely with engineering, application and operational teams, providing broad exposure to diverse technologies and service landscapes. * You will contribute to improvement initiatives that refine our processes, automation and data quality — allowing you to shape how we work. * You’ll join a supportive practice where knowledge\-sharing, collaboration and professional growth are prioritised. Growth and development opportunities * Build deep expertise in ITIL 4 Change Enablement and ServiceNow Change Management. * Gain exposure to impact assessments, risk evaluation, and leading structured decision\-making forums. * Collaborate with our Senior Service Configuration \& Change Specialist and Service Configuration Specialist, broadening your understanding of CMDB, CSDM, and service mapping. * Contribute to enhancements in automation, workflow design, and Change governance. * Access Arup’s learning resources, mentoring and global communities to grow your career in ITSM. Key responsibilities * Manage and coordinate all Change Requests through the full lifecycle, ensuring accuracy, risk awareness and quality documentation. * Review submissions and engage with stakeholders to ensure changes are well‑assessed and ready for implementation. * Chair or facilitate CAB/ECAB meetings, ensuring effective preparation, discussion and follow‑up. * Maintain the Forward Schedule of Change, ensuring visibility and coordination of upcoming work. * Maintain high‑quality, audit‑ready change records and contribute to change performance and trend reporting. * Collaborate with technical teams to understand impacts, dependencies and readiness for change. * Contribute to practice improvements such as workflow optimisation, automation or reporting enhancements. * Support integration between Change Enablement and Configuration Management by ensuring change data aligns with CI/service data. * Assist with basic CMDB/CSDM activities when needed (e.g., CI data validation, service mapping checks). What a typical day might look like * Reviewing new Change submissions, assessing quality, and coordinating with technical teams for clarifications or updates. * Preparing the CAB agenda, chairing a Virtual CAB session and recording decisions and required actions. * Updating the FSC, validating dependencies or scheduling considerations and communicating changes to stakeholders. * Investigating a failed or emergency change, helping to coordinate corrective actions and documenting insights. * Working with the Senior Specialist on an improvement initiative, such as refining risk scoring or testing workflow enhancements in ServiceNow. * Collaborating with the Service Configuration Specialist to cross‑check CI data associated with upcoming changes. **Did you know:** Our Digital Technology team are proud prior winners of the IT Team of the Year Award at the Chartered Institute of IT’s UK IT Industry Awards. **Is this role right for you?** * Experience in Change Enablement, with confidence managing Change Requests throughout their lifecycle. * Ability to chair CAB/ECAB meetings, facilitating constructive discussions and supporting informed decisions. * Hands‑on familiarity with ServiceNow, including Change workflows and reporting. * Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, able to guide discussions and explain impacts clearly. * A detail‑focused, organised and improvement‑oriented mindset, with a desire to enhance process maturity. **What We Offer You** At Arup, we care about each member’s success, so we can grow together. Guided by our values, we provide an attractive total reward package that recognises the contribution of each of our members to our shared success. As well as competitive, fair and equitable pay, we offer a career in which all of our members can belong, grow and thrive – through benefits that support health and wellbeing, a wide range of learning opportunities and many possibilities to have an impact through the work they do. We are owned in trust on behalf of our members, giving us the freedom, with personal responsibility, to set our own direction and choose work that aligns with our purpose and adds to Arup’s legacy. Our members collaborate on ambitious projects to deliver remarkable outcomes for our clients and communities. Profit Share is a key part of our reward, enabling members to share in the results of our collective efforts. **Different people, shared values** Arup is an equal opportunity employer that actively promotes and nurtures a diverse and inclusive workforce. We welcome applications from individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, marital status, disability, neurotype or mental health, race or ethnicity, faith or belief, sexual orientation, socioeconomic background, and whether you’re pregnant or on family leave. We are an open environment that embraces diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas – this drives our excellence. Guided by our values and alignment with the UN Sustainable Development Goals, we create and contribute to equitable spaces and systems, while cultivating a sense of belonging for all. Our internal employee networks support our inclusive culture: from race, ethnicity and cross\-cultural working to gender equity and LGBTQ\+ and disability inclusion – we aim to create a space for you to express yourself and make a positive difference. Discover more about life at Arup at Careers \- Your Life at Arup . We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible to all candidates. Please let us know if you need any assistance or reasonable adjustments throughout your application or interview process, and/or to perform the essential functions of the role. We will do everything we can to support you. **Our Application Process** To understand what to expect next, please visit our Careers page Our aim is to make this process as streamlined and easy as possible for our applicants. Please note that all applicants must apply directly via the job portal and applications sent via email will not be considered. If this role is not quite what you are looking for, but you are interested in other opportunities for a future with purpose, please sign up to our Talent Community where you will be kept up to date with roles suitable for you to shape a better world. Stay safe online \- Arup will never ask for payment or your bank details as part of our recruitment process.

title: Client Associate  - Zenith
description: **The Role** **Overview** Media Teams are our client facing teams in Core responsible for excellence in client service, leading strategy development and managing the media plan. The purpose of this role is to support the Client Managers and Senior Client Managers in the day to day running of client accounts; and handle the administration of excellent media delivery across many clients. It requires the use of media planning tools, the effective management of the media planning processes, excellent attention to detail for accurate reporting and time management to ensure best in class delivery on behalf of the media team for clients. It also requires strong collaboration and communication with the wider organisation across the Central Team to ensure that delivery is to the highest standards. A strong working relationship with colleagues, particularly the Channel Planning and Investment teams, is a crucial element of this role. Media Team Client Associates have a very structured learning programme in place, delivered by the best trainers in the industry. The purpose of this is to ensure a consistent level of knowledge covering everything from the basics to advanced areas of media planning. Quality control of output will be critical to this role. **Responsibilities/ Key Performance Indicators (KPI’s)** Central to this role is a clear understanding of media planning principles and an ability to interpret plans at a high level. This role is central to the efficient and effective running of the day\-to\-day client service team, including working with the Client Manager to help with the actualisation of media plans, monitoring of POs, timely and accurate billing, using agency tools to develop audience and competitive reporting, creating presentations of competitor insights, ideation and working with the team to deliver innovation. As part of this, our Client Associates will have a specific role in helping to execute each team’s Client Satisfaction Plans, which would be defined depending on the client team. The Client Associate will work closely with the Channel Planning and Investment teams, will manage the delivery of the media plan, and will ensure that it is aligned with the overall media rationale. This will all be delivered accurately and by adhering to processes as outlined in our ways of working. KPIs will include unbilled %, efficient resolution of media queries, accuracy of reporting and “Delivery” client service scores in the annual RADAR service review. **AGENCY PERFORMANCE** * Generating reports that deliver actionable insight for clients e.g. using tools such as Adintel, TGI, Techedge etc. * Listens actively to understand client needs * Assisting in the creation and development world class media strategies that can be turned into media plans that are based on best practice planning as taught through Core Learning * Working with the Client Manager on the management of campaign from start to end, essentially project managing the campaign (dealing with client queries, coordinating reports, monitoring, ensuring plans are audit ready, liaising with the digital specialists to monitor campaign delivery, etc) * Learning about all media channels and the rate at which they contribute to the overall plan Internal coordination with Core Practices and Central Teams on live campaign queries including delivery updates, trans times and pacing reports * Update clients on key peak spots in launch week of activity * Develop reports on both routine and ad hoc basis, as well as implementing recommendations from Client Manager/Senior Client Manager based on the data * Liaising with the Creative Agency and OOH agencies for delivery of creative assets * Develop an understanding of each Practice and Central Team within Core and what it contributes to the overall plan * Validate campaign delivery and performance * Help to project manage post\-campaign reviews for clients, as well as populate information into client\-friendly presentation * Helping prep media presentations * Helping to coordinate media owner briefs and delivery * Working with the Client Manager to ensure all plans are audit ready each month * Adopt and embrace an accuracy culture by having and supporting excellent attention to detail * Where required, screenshots of digital activity, or page positions in press may also need to be monitored and reported on **COMMERCIAL PERFORMANCE** * Helping with the collection and reconciliation of client ensuring effective and efficient month end billing in conjunction with the Media Operations Associate * Helping with reconciliation of all media plans at month end to reflect delivery * Main responsibility for billing at the end of each month (reviewed by team) * Ensure all billing is 100% accurate and on time for clients, and particularly on new out of scope projects, with correct booking category being used to easily and accurately identify progress. * Ensure timesheets are updated weekly and that they accurately reflect the time spent on our clients’ businesses. **TALENT MANAGEMENT** * Commit to the internal Core Learning programme(s) and the defined training track to be completed in your first year; and regularly attend training for your development proactively, outside of formal scheduled training time * Helping to drive and build culture, creating a fun working and learning environment * Collaborate closely with the Client Director to ensure your personal development requirements are met * Ensure a strong comprehension and commitment to your career development plan in line with your 360 appraisal, quarterly catch up’s and goals * Uphold Core’s values in words and actions * Proactively build relationships within the wider company **Your profile** **Experience** * No previous experience of media planning is required but an interest in media planning and buying is essential * Good working knowledge of Microsoft Office suite, particularly Excel and PowerPoint * High level of business acumen **Person Specification** * Be energetic and efficient in approach * Actively display a passion for the industry * Strong attention to detail * Show curiosity in learning new things at a fast pace every day * Excellent interpersonal skills\- ability to build strong relationships with colleagues across Core, clients and media owners * Display the ability to think innovatively * Understands the importance of upskilling * Understands and appreciates the benefit of processes * Be responsive to requests * Know when to escalate and involve other people **Why us?** **What’s in it for you?** * Work your way – enjoy flexible, hybrid working that fits around your life * Never stop growing – 24/7 access to LinkedIn Learning, plus clear career progression and mentoring support * Variety that excites – collaborate with an incredible mix of clients across sport, retail, food \& drink, alcohol, insurance, banking and more * Grow with us – real opportunities to develop and progress within the business * Time to recharge – 21 days annual leave, your birthday off, Christmas closure days, and early finishes on bank holiday Fridays * Balance matters – a culture that genuinely supports work–life balance * People\-first perks – employee referral programme and family\-focused benefits, including paid maternity \& paternity leave, fertility support, pregnancy loss leave, and a maternity phase\-back programme * Your wellbeing, covered – access to mental health and wellbeing programmes, plus paid health insurance for you and your dependents under 18 * Plan for the future – professional financial advice, contributory pension, and life assurance * Be part of something bigger – join our D\&I, CSR, Green, Social or Great Place to Work committees and even earn an extra day’s leave for making an impact * And plenty more along the way… **Salary Range** 30000 \-31000 DOE **About Us** Core is Ireland's largest marketing communications company with over 400 people working across the key areas of marketing communications \- research, data science, strategy, planning, creative development and implementation. We are now a collective of nine practices, working towards a single purpose \- to expand the possibilities of what brands can achieve. At Core, we believe that change can move us forward and compel us towards new possibilities. Our wonderful people are at the centre of our success. It is essential that we keep building a high\-trust working environment that supports our people and enables them to flourish. There is no magic formula for becoming a great place to work. Instead, it involves working together, through constant change, to match the personal dreams and ambitions of our people with the aspirations of the organisation. Over the years, we have cemented our position as a great place to work, with 2025 being voted 8 Best Large Workplace in Ireland. Additionally, we are recognised as a Best Workplace for Women, and Best Workplace for Health \& Wellbeing. 2025 was our best year yet, and we're excited to have you join us and our continued success.

insurance, banking and more * Grow with us – real opportunities to develop and progress within the business * Time to recharge – 21 days annual leave, your birthday off, Christmas closure days, and early finishes on bank holiday Fridays * Balance matters – a culture that genuinely supports work–life balance * People\-first perks – employee referral programme and family\-focused benefits, including paid maternity \& paternity leave, fertility support, pregnancy loss leave, and a maternity phase\-back programme * Your wellbeing, covered – access to mental health and wellbeing programmes, plus paid health insurance for you and your dependents under 18 * Plan for the future – professional financial advice, contributory pension, and life assurance * Be part of something bigger – join our D\&I, CSR, Green, Social or Great Place to Work committees and even earn an extra day’s leave for making an impact * And plenty more along the way… #### **Salary Range** 30000 \-31000 DOE #### **About us** Core is Ireland's largest marketing communications company with over 400 people working across the key areas of marketing communications \- research, data science, strategy, planning, creative development and implementation. We are now a collective of nine practices, working towards a single purpose \- to expand the possibilities of what brands can achieve. At Core, we believe that change can move us forward and compel us towards new possibilities. Our wonderful people are at the centre of our success. It is essential that we keep building a high\-trust working environment that supports our people and enables them to flourish. There is no magic formula for becoming a great place to work. Instead, it involves working together, through constant change, to match the personal dreams and ambitions of our people with the aspirations of the organisation. Over the years, we have cemented our position as a great place to work, with 2025 being voted 8 Best Large Workplace in Ireland. Additionally, we are recognised as a Best Workplace for Women, and Best Workplace for Health \& Wellbeing. 2025 was our best year yet, and we're excited to have you join us and our continued success.

title: Coca-Cola Ignite Intern - PACS
description: **Job Description** **Function Overview at Coca\-Cola** Public Affairs, Communications, and Sustainability (PACS) at Coca\-Cola leads the company’s efforts to engage with communities, media, and stakeholders while advancing our sustainability and social responsibility initiatives. We are committed to making a positive impact on society and the environment. **What will you do as a Coca\-Cola Ignite Intern?** * Gain hands\-on experience, develop critical skills, and handle complex projects with real business impact. * Get an opportunity accelerate your career growth within Coca\-Cola's global networked organization. * Network with leaders and colleagues, learning from their expertise through various engagement opportunities. * Be part of a vibrant global community of interns and early career professionals, sharing experiences and insights. * Contribute to meaningful initiatives promoting social impact and sustainability, making a positive impact in our communities **Key Responsibilities** As a PACS Intern, you will play an important role in supporting Coca\-Cola’s public relations, sustainability, and communication strategies. You will work on projects that promote environmental responsibility, foster community relations, and ensure that our brand’s message is communicated clearly and effectively. * Assist in developing and implementing public relations strategies that enhance Coca\-Cola’s reputation. * Support sustainability programs and corporate social responsibility (CSR) initiatives. * Monitor and track media coverage to assess public perception and reputation. * Help coordinate communication campaigns and community outreach programs. * Collaborate with internal teams to align sustainability efforts with company values. * Analyze stakeholder feedback and recommend improvements. **Requirements** * Educational background: An undergraduate student, pursuing a bachelor's degree, or master’s student, completing a master’s degree in communications, Public Affairs, Environmental Science, or related field. * Right to work: Legal authorisation to work in Ireland. No visa sponsorship is provided. * Commitment: Must be available to start October 1st, 2026, and commit to 36\.25 hours/week for a 12\-month internship. * Language: Fluency in English (with strong verbal and written communication skills) * Attributes: Enthusiasm towards Coca\-Cola, its brands and its values. Curious, agile, creative and passionate about learning and making a true impact in a high\-performance and diverse environment. * Relationship skills: Being inclusive, team\-oriented, and adept at building relationships. * Relevant Knowledge: Excellent writing and verbal communication skills. Passion for sustainability and community initiatives, with strong ethical standards. Strong understanding of sustainability practices and public relations. Proficiency in social media and content creation. Creative and strategic thinking. **Location(s):** Ireland **City/Cities** Dublin **Travel Required** 00% \- 25% **Relocation Provided:** No **Job Posting End Date** September 29, 2026 **Our Purpose And Growth Culture** We are taking deliberate action to nurture an inclusive culture that is grounded in our company purpose, to refresh the world and make a difference. We act with a growth mindset, take an expansive approach to what’s possible and believe in continuous learning to improve our business and ourselves. We focus on four key behaviors – curious, empowered, inclusive and agile – and value how we work as much as what we achieve. We believe that our culture is one of the reasons our company continues to thrive after 130\+ years. Visit Our Purpose and Vision to learn more about these behaviors and how you can bring them to life in your next role at Coca\-Cola.

title: Company Secretary
description: Our client, a leading Law Firm based in Dublin 2 is looking for a fully qualified Company Secretary to join their large team. The role offers opportunities for advancement, good work life balance and an excellent package **Call Cormac Spencer now on 01 845 4651 for more info!** **Salary:** to €60k **Benefits:** Pension, Bonus, Gym, Canteen, Travel Tax Saver Scheme, 2 days WFH and more **Total Package:** up to €80k **Key Responsibilities:** * Ensure the maintenance of company secretarial records, including Minute Books and Statutory Registers. * To liaise with external stakeholders as well as being the point of contact for client companies and their board of directors. * To manage the proper handling of both corporate and financial transactions of clients. * To ensure all other requirements under the Companies Act 2014 are met. * Attend shareholder and board meetings, recording and circulating minutes. * To maintain and grow relationships with existing clients and intermediaries. * Monitor transaction of the companies under management in accordance with the transaction monitoring process and procedure. * Manage and coordinate the reporting of any legal issues with clients. * To ensure client files are in accordance with the filing and archiving processes and update models. * Assist with the training of Junior team members when required. **The Candidate:** * 3\+ years' experience as a Company Secretary. * Previous experience working in a corporate services provider/law firm. * Part / Fully CGI Qualified. * Experience attending board meetings and drafting minutes. * Ability to use own initiative and be adaptable. * Good attention to detail and very organised individual. **If this Company Secretary Role Sounds Like You, Call Cormac NOW on 01 845 4651\.** **Speak to the Experts with the Best Company Secretary Jobs in Ireland** **Link Personnel** **01 845 4651**

title: Consultant - Operation Analyst

Our clients value our proactive approach, depth of expertise, integrity and the quality we deliver. As a result, our people get to enjoy working on some of the most exciting projects in the world. **Job Description** Due to growing demand for our consultancy services, we are seeking a consultant responsible for the configuration administration, and ongoing optimisation of project management systems and associated reporting tools in Power BI. The role ensures the stability, accuracy, and usability of the project management environment, supports users across the organisation and maintains dashboards that provide real‑time performance insight for projects, programmes, and portfolios. **Key Responsibilities** * Configure, maintain and optimise project management systems and related features * Manage workflows, custom fields, lookup tables and security permissions * Monitor system performance, perform routine housekeeping and ensure high platform availability * Coordinate with client’s IT team on patching, updates and system‑related issues * Maintain and enhance dashboards in Power BI (or equivalent BI tools) * Ensure data refresh schedules, connections and gateway configurations are reliable and secure * Provide day‑to‑day user support, troubleshooting and issue resolution for users * Develop training materials, user guides, and process documentation * Conduct onboarding and refresher training sessions for project managers, schedulers and PMO teams * Identify opportunities to enhance system functionality, user experience, and data quality **Qualifications** * Minimum of 1 year of experience in administering and maintaining systems and dashboards * Bachelor’s degree in STEM related subjects, e.g. Mathematics, Computer Science * Good knowledge of construction industry technical matters, such as procurement, design, construction and handover * Strong understanding of project, programme, and portfolio management processes * Hands\-on experience in administering project management systems (e.g. MS Project Web App, Oracle Primavera Cloud, etc.) * Hands\-on experience in reporting tools such as Power BI Desirable Skills and Accreditations * Knowledge of PMO frameworks (PRINCE2, APM, PMI) * Familiarity with SQL, DAX, or data modelling **Additional Information** Our people share our Purpose and Values. title: Customer Experience Champion - Mullingar
description: PTSB is one of Ireland's leading retail and SME banks, with an innovative range of products and services powered through an evolving digital landscape, our focus is centred on ensuring we deliver what our customers, colleagues and communities need to be successful. As a Branch Customer Experience Champion, you will work collaboratively with your team and Branch Lead to support the efficient delivery of business objectives and the day to day branch operations in a compliant manner. You will actively develop your skills through participation in our Collaborative Customer Conversations program whilst ensuring you meet the needs of our customers through promotion of our Omni\-Channel approach. You will become familiar with the Banks Customer Segmentation Strategy and take part in \`in branch' and localised promotional activities within the territory to identify new business opportunities to increase new customer acquisition whilst maintaining positive relationships with existing customers. **Responsibilities:** * Support in the day to day operation of customer service within the branch. * Provide an excellent level of customer service and advise our customers throughout their product /financial needs journey, further improving the customer experience with both over the phone and face to face interactions. * Take ownership and deal with customer queries in an effective, professional and compliant manner. * Generate and execute sales from lead (where qualified to do so) to fulfilment in accordance with the Omni\-Channel ethos and activity management system. * Assist with sales campaigns including post sales fulfilment, administration and follow up tasks. * Adopt a prompt and customer centred response to leads passed from Open24 to maximise new business opportunities from customer base. **Requirements:** * QFA or APA in Loans and/or Savings \& Investments, with up\-to\-date CPD hours for the relevant CPD years. If no APA held, or only one APA in Loans or S\&I held, the candidate must commit to qualifying within a 2 year period to achieve APA in both Loans and S\&I. * If no APA held, the candidate must meet the minimum entry requirements of holding an Ordinary Leaving Certificate (or equivalent) with a grade D3/O6/H6 at Ordinary or Higher Level in five Leaving Certificate subjects (including English and Maths), and/or 5\+ years post Leaving Certificate experience. * Strong interpersonal and communication skills with a commitment to providing an outstanding customer experience * Have significant level of proven sales, customer service or clerical experience in a regulatory compliant environment (where MCC) * Committed to and enjoys working in a sales environment **This is a Permanent role, based in Mullingar (Onsite).** Is this you? Please apply online on our website or via the apply link of this role. Your application will be sent through to our Talent Acquisition team and they will be in touch regarding your application. We reserve the right to draw up a shortlist for interview. The Bank understands the importance of a consistent and relentless focus on championing diversity and inclusion. We aim to attract, recruit, and retain individuals with diverse backgrounds, skills, competencies and abilities to work collaboratively to enhance the service we provide to all of our customers and the communities we serve.

title: Customer Support Agent&KYC Analyst (European Language Speaking)
description: We are a dynamic and rapidly growing company operating in the fintech and payments industry, delivering innovative digital solutions to customers worldwide. We are now looking for a talented **Customer Support Agent \& KYC Analyst (European Languages)** to join our growing team in **Dublin** . **Responsibilities:** * Handle customer complaints, provide timely and appropriate solutions, and follow up to ensure resolution via chat, email, and occasional phone support. * Maintain detailed records of customer interactions, inquiries, comments, and complaints. * Troubleshoot customer issues and escalate to the appropriate teams when necessary, using platforms like Slack. * Deliver excellent customer service by responding to customer enquiries in a timely and professional manner. * Review and analyse customer information to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and company policies. * Conduct thorough due diligence checks on new and existing customers to ensure compliance with KYC and AML regulations. **Requirements:** * **Fluent proficiency in both spoken and written English and at least one European language** * Strong problem\-solving skills with the ability to provide effective solutions to customers * Prior experience in customer\-oriented roles * Prior experience in KYC roles is highly desirable but not essential * Excellent written and verbal communication skills * Familiarity with customer service software and tools * Ability to work independently and as part of a team in a fast\-paced environment * Willingness to work flexible hours, including weekends and bank holidays, to support customers in different time zones **Skills and Experience** * High school diploma or equivalent; university degree preferred * 1\+ years of experience in customer service, preferably in a global support environment * **Experience in KYC, fraud, fintech, or other regulated environments is highly desirable** * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills * Strong customer service skills, including empathy, patience, and problem\-solving abilities

title: Cyber Security Analyst
description: My Dublin based client is recruiting for a Cyber Security Risk Analyst to join a growing team on a permanent basis. This role requires 1 day per week onsite. The Cyber Security Risk Analyst will be responsible for establishing and maintaining a strong security posture across the organisation. The team defines security strategies, frameworks, and controls, and ensures their effective implementation to protect information assets and manage security risks. **Responsibilities** * Act as a primary point of contact for information security matters related to service delivery and operational activities. * Promote awareness and understanding of information security principles, policies, and processes across the organisation. * Identify, assess, and monitor information security risks in collaboration with risk owners, ensuring appropriate controls are applied throughout the service and system lifecycle. * Evaluate the effectiveness of information security controls and ensure alignment with organisational policies, regulatory obligations, and recognised industry standards. * Develop, maintain, and improve information security policies, standards, and procedures. * Support third\-party risk management activities, including defining and assessing security requirements for suppliers and outsourced services. * Lead or contribute to security initiatives and improvement projects, working with cross\-functional teams to enhance security processes and increase automation where appropriate. **Skills / Experience Required** * A degree in computer science, information technology, or a related discipline, or equivalent professional experience. * 5\+ years of experience in information security or related IT roles, with strong exposure to risk management, security frameworks, and control design. * Broad knowledge of information security technologies, architectures, and operational processes, including information security management systems and industry standards (e.g. ISO 27001, COBIT, DORA, NIS2\). * Experience working with data, reporting, automation, or AI\-enabled tools to support security processes. * Experience managing or assessing security risks associated with third\-party suppliers, cloud services, and outsourced environments. * Proven ability to manage projects and influence stakeholders in complex organisational environments. * Strong communication skills, with the ability to produce clear, professional documentation and communicate effectively with technical and non\-technical audiences. **Benefits** Hybrid Working \- Only 1 Day per week required on site Healthcare Bonus Childcare Allowance Pension Sub Canteen

title: Data Analyst
description: **ROLE:** Data Analyst **LOCATION: Dublin, Perm, Hybrid** ***ABOUT THE ROLE:*** Barden are delighted to be partnering with our Client on a new opportunity for an **experienced Data Analyst** . This role sits within their Group Commercial Team and will someone with circa 4\-5 years’ experience across data storytelling, dashboard development and commercial performance. This role will support strategic decision making by delivering actionable insights through Power BI dashboard reporting, working closely with internal stakeholders across the Group. **Key Responsibilities:** The core responsibilities will centre around the following areas, and will evolve depending on the needs of the business: * Develop, maintain, and optimise interactive dashboards and reports using Power BI to support commercial decision\-making. * Deliver commercial insights and reporting at a group level, ensuring alignment across all regions. * Collaborate with internal stakeholders to gather requirements, understand business needs, and translate them into actionable data solutions. * Assist in the development of automated reporting processes. * Contribute to ad\-hoc analysis and deep dives to uncover trends, risks, and opportunities. * Support the cross\-functional taskforce focused on aligning KPIs across regions, contributing to consistency and comparability of commercial performance metrics. * Ensure data governance and compliance with internal standards. ***ABOUT THE PERSON:*** **Technical Skills:** * Strong proficiency in Power BI and its components (e.g. DAX, Power Query) essential. * Experience working with large datasets and data modelling. * Knowledge of SQL, or Python is a plus. * Ability to translate business requirements into effective data visualisations. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills, with excellent attention to detail. * Commercially minded individual, with a strong understanding of how data and insights drive business performance and decision\-making. * Excellent communication skills and ability to present insights to non\-technical audience.

title: Data Analyst
description: **About The Role** We are seeking a highly analytical and detail\-oriented Data Analyst to join our team. In this role, you will be responsible for transforming complex data into understandable and conclusive insights. You will play a key role in training different business areas on the effective use of our Business Intelligence (BI) tools and advising them on how to maximize the value derived from our data. If you have a passion for data storytelling, strong communication skills, and enjoy empowering others with data, we encourage you to apply. **Responsibilities** * Collect, clean, analyze, and interpret large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and insights. * Create clear, concise, and impactful visualizations, reports, and dashboards that effectively communicate findings to various stakeholders. * Develop and deliver training sessions to educate different business units on the functionality and best practices of BI tools. * Provide expert advice and guidance to the business area on how to leverage data to answer key business questions and drive informed decision\-making. * Collaborate with data engineers and other teams to ensure data accuracy, accessibility, and consistency. * Identify opportunities for process improvements and data optimization. * Stay up\-to\-date with industry best practices and emerging trends in data analysis, BI tools, and data visualization. **Qualifications** * Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field such as Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, Business Analytics, or a Business related discipline. * Proven experience as a Data Analyst or in a similar analytical role. * Strong proficiency in SQL. * Extensive experience with Business Intelligence (BI) tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, Looker, Qlik Sense). * Excellent data visualization skills and the ability to present complex information clearly and concisely. * Strong analytical, problem\-solving, and critical thinking abilities. * Exceptional communication and presentation skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non\-technical audiences. * Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast\-paced environment. * Detail\-oriented with a strong commitment to data accuracy and integrity.

title: Design Project Engineer - Mechanical, Fleet Remediation Engineering
description: **Description** As an Amazon Project Design Mechanical Engineer, you will lead design projects in operational data centers, that remediate issues, upgrade our existing infrastructure or add new services. You will be the 'go to' engineering resource for these projects and will provide lifecycle support from design through implementation. You will have the opportunity to work on projects locally and globally. **You Will Use Your Mechanical Expertise To** * Own the design for existing data center upgrades and design\-solutions which add data center infrastructure, capacity, improve availability, and/or increase sustainability. * Perform design and equipment submittal review for Data Centers in your region. * Interface with internal and external data center design engineering team, server hardware team,environmental health and safety team, field engineering, and controls engineering to promote standards that maintain consistency and reliability in services delivered. * Work on concurrent projects, sometimes in multiple geographical regions. * Act as technical subject matter expert for your given discipline during new remediation projects. * Support construction, commissioning, operations, and turnover. Amazon's vision is to be the world's most customer\-centric company, and this role is key to that vision. As a Fleet Remediation Project Design Mechanical Engineer, you will be leading projects to fit out our data centers to meet ever\-evolving customer needs as we continue expanding our fleet to hyper\-scale. **About AIS** AWS Infrastructure Services (AIS) owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help. You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion. Key job responsibilities * Perform design and equipment submittal review for Data Centers in your region. * Interface with internal and external data center design engineering team, server hardware team, environmental health and safety team, field engineering, and controls engineering to promote standards that maintain consistency and reliability in services delivered. * Work on concurrent projects, sometimes in multiple geographical regions. * Act as a technical subject matter expert for your given discipline during new remediation projects. * Support construction, commissioning, operations, and turnover. * Possess strong engineering judgement and are able to provide design and recommendations despite uncertainty * Are detail and data oriented with familiarity with design/construction processes * Have experience managing engineering projects and engineering consultants. * Build trust and relationships with different stakeholders (e.g., Operations, Commissioning, Construction and Design) * Manage design and as built drawings and documents * Prepare and present progress reports to all relevant stakeholders * Be inclined to get into the field to see things up close A day in the life Each day you will interact with different teams responsible for various aspects of the data centers. You will mentor and oversee the work of junior engineers in your discipline. You will have the opportunity to work on projects locally and globally, with up to 25% travel. **About The Team** Our Design Engineers drive data center infrastructure projects with strong engineering judgment, attention to detail, and hands\-on field experience. They manage projects and consultants, build cross\-functional relationships, oversee design documentation, and prioritize initiatives that maximize impact on safety, capacity, and availability. Why AWS? Inclusive Team Culture Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee\-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness. Mentorship \& Career Growth We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional. Work/Life Balance We value work\-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud. Inclusive Team Culture AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee\-led and company\-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do. **Basic Qualifications** * Knowledge of mechanical or electrical systems design * Experience reading, interpreting, and creating construction drawings, specifications, and submittal documents * Experience in industrial or commercial engineering in mission critical facilities including but not limited to: data centers, power generation or oil and gas facilities * Bachelor's degree in Mechanical or Electrical Engineering or a related field **Preferred Qualifications** * Professional Engineer License * Experience with building codes and regulations for your region * Experience carrying design concepts through exploration, development, and into deployment or mass production Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates. Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner. **Company** \- Amazon Data Services Ireland Limited Job ID: A3183151

title: DevOps BizOps Engineer
description: Job Description* Job Title: DevOps BizOps Engineer City: Dublin State/Province: Dublin Posting Start Date: 4/7/26 Wipro Limited (NYSE: WIT, BSE: 507685, NSE: WIPRO) is a leading technology services and consulting company focused on building innovative solutions that address clients’ most complex digital transformation needs. Leveraging our holistic portfolio of capabilities in consulting, design, engineering, and operations, we help clients realize their boldest ambitions and build future\-ready, sustainable businesses. With over 230,000 employees and business partners across 65 countries, we deliver on the promise of helping our customers, colleagues, and communities thrive in an ever\-changing world. For additional information, visit us at www.wipro.com. Job Description: **Job Description** ------------------- As a BizOps Senior Engineer, you will act as the production readiness steward for versatile Gateway products and integration with other platforms. You will partner with development teams to design, implement, and support services with a focus on operational resilience, automation, and compliance. Should have knowledge of Payment domains specially in Gateways with least gRPC experience and strong with SRE fundamentals and hands\-on experience **Key Responsibilities** * Lifecycle Ownership: Engage in and improve the entire service lifecycle—from design and deployment to operations and continuous improvement. * Operational Readiness: Ensure system availability, capacity, performance, monitoring, and self\-healing capabilities are embedded throughout delivery. * Incident Management: Practice sustainable incident response, lead blameless postmortems, and optimize Mean Time to Recovery (MTTR). * Develop and maintain automation pipelines for certificate renewal, traffic routing, alerting, and compliance reporting using tools like Ansible, Venafi \& XLR template. * Support CI/CD pipelines for software promotion and operational gating. * Reliability Engineering: Scale systems sustainably through automation and advocate for changes that improve reliability and velocity. * Compliance \& Risk Management: Drive initiatives for Safety \& Soundness, PCI compliance, threat/toil reduction, and ITSM defect resolution. * Monitoring \& Observability: Implement robust logging, monitoring, and alerting standards to ensure system health and proactive issue detection. Hands\-on experience with Dynatrace \& Splunk monitoring tool configuration and alerting. * Collaboration: Work with global teams across multiple time zones and mentor junior engineers. Continuous Improvement: Provide feedback loops to development teams on resiliency gaps and operational enhancements. * **Required Experience \& Skills** * Experience: Extensive experience in BizOps, Site Reliability Engineering, or DevOps roles. * Strong understanding of NGINX configuration and gRPC event\-driven architectures. * Proficiency in DevOps tools: Chef, Jenkins, Groovy, shell scripting, Bitbucket, Git, Ansible, XLR. * Experience with AWS infrastructure, secure access practices, and cloud\-native deployments. * Security \& Compliance: * Awareness of certificate lifecycle management, mutual TLS, SSL handshake, SSH keys, encryption standards. * Familiarity with ITSM processes, compliance frameworks, and incident management. * Networking \& Systems: * Knowledge of client\-server relationships, network layers (L1–L7\), load balancers (BIG\-IP F5\), and application firewalls. * Ability to analyze stack traces, TCP dumps, heap/thread dumps, and perform OS\-level troubleshooting. * Authentication \& Authorization: * Intermediate understanding of Active Directory, SAML, LTPA, SSO, OAuth. **͏** ----- **Deliver** **No** **Performance Parameter** **Measure** 1\. Continuous Integration,Deployment \& Monitoring 100% error free on boarding \& implementation 2\. CSAT Timely customer resolution as per TAT Zero escalation **͏** ----- **͏** ----- Mandatory Skills: Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) . Experience: 3\-5 Years . Reinvent your world. We are building a modern Wipro. We are an end\-to\-end digital transformation partner with the boldest ambitions. To realize them, we need people inspired by reinvention. Critical Airflow Europe Ltd represents Phoenix, Strobic and Aircuity.** **Summary of the Role** The aim of the Graduate Programme is to develop the best and brightest engineering talent to become the future leaders. HVAC systems including GxP HVAC Systems, LEV’s and Fume Cupboards and associated utilities systems. * HEPA Filter Integrity Testing * Particle Count testing * Verification of cleanroom air change rates and Cleanroom pressure profiles * HVAC Air and Water Systems Balancing and Commissioning * HVAC Systems Trouble Shooting and Fault Finding * Complete validation documentation, check out sheets Engineering Degree L7/8 \- Mechanical/Electrical/Building Services * Excellent communication skills both written and verbal. * Computer Literate. * Ability to work on own initiative. **Required Skills** * Working knowledge of Microsoft office (excel, word, PowerPoint). * Team player, with a flexible approach but also able to work under own initiative. * Strong verbal and interpersonal skills * Competent level of technical writing and understanding of technical documentation. * Full clean driving licence required. * Must be eligible to work in Ireland. **Benefits** Eirdata offer support that enables you to never stop growing. Your reward at Eirdata is not just your salary, we believe in investing in our people's personal, professional development and empowering their work\-life balance to enable continuous growth. We offer supports and benefits that suit you, wherever you are in life, including Health care, pension, training, coaching. * Competitive Salay * Very attractive pension scheme * VHI * Laptop * Phone * Paid Annual Leave * Supporting your work life balance

title: Electrical Estimator
description: **TDE Group – Electrical Contractors** Kilcullen, Co. Kildare TDE Group are an expanding electrical contractor delivering commercial, industrial and fit\-out projects across Ireland. Our recent work includes warehouse and logistics projects, commercial fit\-outs across Ireland. This is an excellent opportunity for someone with an electrical trade background or construction estimating experience to develop their career within a growing company. Key Responsibilities * Review tender drawings, specifications and project documentation to understand project scope and requirements. * Carry out detailed quantity take\-offs from electrical drawings including lighting, power, containment, distribution, fire alarm and specialist systems. * Assist in preparing detailed cost estimates including labour, materials, plant and subcontract packages. * Issue supplier and subcontractor enquiries, ensuring competitive quotations are obtained within tender deadlines. * Analyse supplier quotations and ensure all items are compliant with the project specification and scope. * Prepare Bills of Quantities and pricing schedules for tender submissions. * Assist in compiling complete tender submissions, including pricing breakdowns, clarifications and supporting documentation. * Maintain and develop an internal cost database including labour rates, material pricing and historical project costs. * Liaise with suppliers, subcontractors and manufacturers to obtain technical and commercial information required for tenders. * Attend site visits and tender walkdowns where required to assess installation conditions and logistics. * Work with the directors to review tender risk, programme durations and commercial considerations. * Support post\-tender clarifications and negotiations with clients or main contractors. * Assist with project handover to the delivery team once contracts are awarded. * Maintain accurate records of tender submissions, pricing information and supplier quotations Requirements * Electrical trade background or construction related qualification preferred * Ability to read and interpret electrical drawings * Strong organisational and communication skills * Good knowledge of Microsoft Excel and Office * Attention to detail and ability to meet deadlines * Full driving licence What We Offer * Salary €35,000 – €45,000 depending on experience * Opportunity to progress into a senior estimating role * Exposure to commercial and industrial electrical projects * A supportive working environment within a growing contractor * Location: Kilcullen, Co. Kildare * Apply by sending CV to: Info@TDEGroup.ie Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €35,000\.00\-€45,000\.00 per year Work Location: In person

title: Executive Associate (Strategy, Data & Delivery)
description: The Executive Associate, DCEO Office (Operations \& Insights) is a high‑impact, early‑career role that blends executive support, data analysis, project delivery, and board‑level storytelling. Partnering directly with the DCEO, you will orchestrate the rhythm of the business, secure timely, high‑quality inputs from functions and service providers, and transform complex data into crisp narratives and decision materials. The role flexes with the CEO’s priorities week‑to‑week, ensuring momentum, clarity, and results. **Key Responsibilities** * Run the DCEO Rhythm of the Business: own the annual, quarterly, monthly, and weekly cadence, including inputs, deadlines, and deliverables. * Board \& ExComm reporting: shape storylines, synthesise metrics, and produce clear, visually compelling packs that “bring the requisite audience on a journey” without overloading figures. * Insight generation: translate operational and financial data into trends, risks, opportunities, and recommended actions. * Data pipeline coordination: secure timely, complete, and high‑quality data from functions and external service providers; resolve gaps and inconsistencies fast. * Presentation excellence: build executive‑level slides and visuals in PowerPoint, uphold a consistent design system and narrative structure. * Project management: plan, track, and deliver DCEO‑sponsored initiatives, manage scope and comms. * Stakeholder orchestration: align C\-Suite colleagues, Directors, Finance, HR, Medical, Clinical/Ops, IT, and external partners around DCEO priorities. * Decision support: prepare succinct decision memos (“one‑pagers”), options, trade‑offs, and clear recommendations. * Executive briefings: package pre‑reads and talking points for internal and external meetings; ensure pre‑wire with key stakeholders. * Follow‑through: capture meeting actions, their owners, and due dates, then drive to closure and surface slippage early. * Issue escalation: spot signals in sow downs or failures, escalate the right issues to the DCEO with context and proposed fixes. * Confidentiality \& governance: handle sensitive information with discretion, follow information‑security and approvals protocols. * Calendar and priority triage: protect DCEO focus time, sequence work to match shifting priorities. * Vendor/service‑provider management: set expectations, SLAs, and delivery quality for data and content inputs. * KPI / dashboard upkeep: maintain a concise “single source of truth” for top metrics and update cadence. * Change agility: rapidly re‑plan when priorities move, communicate impacts and next steps. * Process improvement: simplify reporting cycles and automate where practical (templates, checklists, light scripts). * Internal communications: draft professional crisp updates from DCEO office as required. * Knowledge management: maintain a searchable, version‑controlled repository of past packs, decisions, and templates. * Culture \& standards: model pace, professionalism, and high bar for quality across all deliverables. **Person Requirements** * Early‑career track record (typically 2–4 years) in one or more of: analytics, strategy/ops, PMO, management consulting, Human Resources or high‑performing EA/Chief‑of‑Staff‑associate roles. * Outstanding PowerPoint: visual storytelling, executive‑level slide craft, and narrative design. * Strong quantitative literacy: Excel/Sheets for analysis; comfort with BI outputs (Power BI/Tableau) and manipulating datasets. * Project delivery: plans, stakeholder management, and a reputation for closing actions. * Executive presence \& writing: concise briefs, decision memos, and clear meeting pre‑reads. * Stakeholder savvy: confident with senior leaders, can secure inputs without formal authority. * Bias for action: self‑starter, organised, and calm under shifting priorities and short notice. * High integrity \& discretion handling confidential information. **Desirable Requirements** * Exposure to board reporting or investor‑style narratives. * Power BI/Tableau basics; familiarity with SharePoint/OneDrive for structured content management. * PMP/PRINCE2, Agile foundations, or analytics/data‑viz coursework/certifications. * Experience working with external vendors/service providers and SLAs. **Qualifications** * Bachelor’s degree (business, economics, analytics, engineering, or related) or equivalent practical experience. THE MATER PRIVATE IS AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITIES EMPLOYER

title: From Events to Career Progression – Get into Recruitment!
description: **From Events to Career Progression – Get into Recruitment!** * **You’ve proven you can handle pressure — now prove how far you can go** * **Stop resetting after every event — start building long\-term progression** * **Your next role shouldn’t be “another job” — it should be a step up** We’re Access, one of Ireland’s fastest\-growing recruitment companies — and every single leader in our business started in an entry\-level role . **Progression here isn’t a promise. It’s a pattern** . In events, you deliver, reset, and start again, with no clear long\-term progression. In recruitment, your determination builds over time: you’ll own relationships, develop your market, and progress based on performance *. Hannah joined us with no recruitment experience and was promoted to Director just last month, showing exactly what happens when ambition meets a structure designed for growth* . **What’s in it for you?** * **Clear promotion pathway** – you’ll always know what’s needed to reach the next level, giving you control over your progression * **Fast\-track progression** – you’ll be able to move quickly when you perform, without waiting years for opportunities * **Leadership opportunities** – you’ll have the chance to step into management roles and increase your impact * **Increasing responsibility and autonomy** – you’ll grow into a role where your decisions shape outcomes * **A career that builds over time** – you’ll stop starting from zero and start creating long\-term success **What we look for:** * 1 to 3 years of experience as an **Event Coordinator, Event Executive, Project Coordinator, Account Executive, or similar** * Experience working in fast\-paced, deadline\-driven environments * Strong stakeholder management and organisational skills * Ambition to progress and take on more responsibility **Additional Information:** Full\-time, office\-based role in Dublin City Centre, Monday to Friday. In your first 2 years, you can expect to earn an average of €64K\+ OTE, with accelerated progression based on performance. For more information, contact Ines Duarte confidentially at \+353 1 572 118 1 or email ines.duarte@accesshealthcare.ie

title: General Practitioner (GP)  with special interest in Palliative Medicine
description: Title: Hospice Doctor/General Practitioner (GP) with special interest in Palliative Medicine Location: LauraLynn House, Leopardstown, Dublin 18 Hours: Part\-time (18\.5 hrs per week). Consideration will also be given to applicants seeking full time (37 hours per week) Salary/Grade: 18\.5 hrs \- €82,505 gross rising to €83,330 gross from 01 Jun 2026 Equivalent Pro Rata Full\-time salary: €165,010 gross (37 hours) rising to €166,660 gross from 01 Jun 2026 Contract Type: Temporary and/or Permanent Job Closing Date: Tuesday 21st April 2026 **Introduction:** The GP will provide medical care in LauraLynn Children's Hospice ensuring the delivery of a high\-quality patient/service user centred service. Working with medical colleagues, The GP provides direct clinical care and all associated medical related activities, including participation in interdisciplinary meetings, family meetings and other relevant activities. The post holder will be required to liaise with other managers and staff in the LauraLynn Hospice service. The post holder will contribute to overall management of the organisation as well as input as appropriate to the development and implementation of organisational policies and procedures. **Benefits:** * Opportunity to work with a dynamic team focussed on supporting people to live their best possible life. * Free car parking * Pension scheme * Subsidised restuarant * Generous annual leave. * VHI group scheme * Bike to work scheme. * Active wellbeing supports including an in\-house occupational health service and a free 24/7/365 employee assistance programme * Excellent learning and development opportunities * Comprehensive orientation pack * Extensive in\-house person\-centred training programme for nursing, clinical and care staff. * Flexible working options * Access to membership of the HSSCU credit union. **Essential Criteria for the Role:** * MB BCH BAO or equivalent. * Full registration on the specialist division of the Medical Council of Ireland under the speciality of general practice or be eligible to apply to be included on this register, or * Current full registration on an alternative appropriate specialist division of the Medical Council of Ireland under an alternative specialism or eligible to be included on such register * MICGP or MRCPI paediatrics, part 1 or MRCPCH Theory exam. * Clinical experience in: Acute paediatrics either during general practice post graduate training and/or paediatric training. * Completion of at least 4 years post\-internship experience in paediatrics or general practice. * Demonstrate an understanding of the importance of multi\-agency working in the context of management of children with life\-limiting conditions. How to Apply: Applications for this role must be made below. Applications received via email will not be considered. For a copy of the job description and person specification contact our HR Department, E: hr@lauralynn.ie For informal enquiries please contact Dr Sozan Abdelrahman Consultant Paediatrician, on 01 2893151 or Email: sabdelrahman@lauralynn.ie **Should you need assistance or have any special needs when attending for interview, please contact any member of the HR team and reasonable accommodation will be arranged**

title: Graduate Building Services Engineer (Dublin) 2026
description: **Overview** **The Opportunity** Get started on an exciting and meaningful career path, working on innovative, challenging projects and learning from industry experts as part of PM Group’s world leading multi\-discipline team. **Who are PM Group?** PM Group is a leading International engineering, architecture, project and construction management firm. With a network of offices in Europe, Asia and the US and a multi\-discipline team of 3,800 people, we deliver complex, capital projects in the life sciences, food and beverages, mission critical/ICT, advanced manufacturing, energy and environmental sectors. We are 100% employee owned so work together to make our company successful. **Responsibilities** As a Building Services Graduate at PM Group, your day to day role would involve, but would not be limited to the following activities: * Work on exciting projects in the Mission Critical, Pharma, Med Tech, Food, Advance Manufacturing and Energy sectors * Coordination with clients, contractors, vendors and other PM Group departments / offices with regard to scope management , deliverables, time lines, resources and 3rd party interfaces / queries. This could include: * BCAR inspections * Support during construction (contractor queries and submittals) * Assist with commissioning activities * Coordination of design with other disciplines * In conjunction with senior engineers and designers preparation of the following deliverables at various project stages: * Calculations (Heating, Cooling, Utilities, etc) and Utility Load Analysis * Schematics (Air \& Instrument Diagrams, Utility Process \& Instrument Diagrams) * Schedules \& Lists (Equipment, Inline and Terminal items, etc) * Data Sheets (AHUs, Boilers, Chillers, etc) * Drawings (AHU Zoning, Pressurisation, Services General Arrangements) * Pipe Stress Analysis and Fluid Flow modelling * Contract Requisitions (installation contracts, turnkey equipment) * Cost Estimates **Qualifications** * Minimum qualification: level 8 degree, level 9 preferred in Building Services or Mechanical Engineering * Ability to work in a vibrant multi discipline collaborative design environment * Excellent interpersonal and communication skills * Strong work ethic and self\-starter * Flexibility to work both in a design office and on a construction site **What are we offering?** An exciting and meaningful career path, working on innovative, challenging projects and learning from industry experts as part of PM Group’s world leading multi\-discipline team. We offer a competitive salary with opportunities to travel and gain a variety of experiences. We also offer an education assisstance programme for anyone interested in furthering their educationch and chartership routes. It's not all work. At PM Group we work hard, but we make sure to have fun too! Be part of the bigger picture and apply today.

title: Graduate Management Trainee - Drogheda
description: **Overview** We’re Enterprise Mobility. A family\-owned, global mobility leader with a $39 billion turnover, nearly 90,000 team members, and operations in 95 countries. Led by CEO Chrissy Taylor, the third generation of the Taylor family, we’re built on a legacy that gives us the stability to focus on the long\-term success of our people, our customers and our business. **Why join the Graduate Management Training Programme?** As a Graduate Management Trainee, you'll have the freedom and support to explore your leadership potential \- and the opportunity to become a branch manager in one of our retail operations in as little as two years. In fact, most of our senior leaders began their careers in this very role \- including our current CEO. From day one, we’ll invest in you. You’ll be in a supportive environment where you’ll take on real responsibilities and gain invaluable hands\-on experience in customer service, sales, marketing, finance, operations, and more. **We work hard and reward hard work** Your work will be recognised with performance\-based incentives and opportunities for continued promotion. Our unique promote\-from\-within culture means you can keep your career moving forward without having to change organisations. **Award\-winning training and development** Whether you’re building on existing strengths or developing new ones, your growth is our priority. Through classroom learning, on\-the\-job training, and mentorship, you’ll have the tools and support to take the next step – and the one after that. **Our doors are open** As a Graduate Management Trainee, your experience at Enterprise Mobility will be as unique as you because you shape who we are as much as we shape your skills, path and potential. We pride ourselves on opening our doors to a variety of voices, giving way to an experience that’s both inclusive and that inspires diversity of thought. **Responsibilities** From your very first day, you’ll be trusted with real responsibility and exposed to all areas of our business. You’ll develop skills in: **Customer Service:** deliver exceptional experiences by confidently handling reservations, resolving enquiries, and building rapport with a diverse range of customers. **Sales and Marketing:** connect with local business partners, grow your network and develop lasting relationships. **Financial Control and Profitability:** understand the financial mechanics of a successful business, including cost control and interpreting profit and loss statements. **Operations and Logistics:** learn how to manage a fleet, plan strategically, and drive performance. **Leadership and Development:** take the lead in mentoring, training, and developing your own team \- with the opportunity to manage and promote others as you grow. **Qualifications** * A Bachelor’s degree is preferred; however, professional experience can be substituted if applicable. * You must have a full manual Republic of Ireland, UK or EU driving licence, but we do make accommodations for applicants who don’t drive due to a disability. * No drug or alcohol related offence on driving record within the last five years is permitted. **Additional Information** * In accordance with current employment permit regulations, we are unable to support an employment permit application for this role, as it is not eligible under the current system. * Regardless of your socio\-economic background, university attended, subject studied, and degree attained, we will always look at how you perform against our competencies and will judge you on that alone. * Please let us know about any accommodations you may need to participate in our recruitment process. * Please limit your application to only one job posting based on where you live and/or plan to work. Applying to multiple locations will delay your application being processed. * This job posting is for applications within the following location(s): Drogheda

title: Graduate Programme 2026
description: **Windward are now accepting applications for their brand\-new Graduate Management Programme commencing in September 2026\.** The successful applicants will each be placed into a Graduate Manager role in one of our managed hotels across Ireland including ***Farnham Estate Spa \& Golf Resort, Aghadoe Heights Hotel \& Spa, Mount Wolseley Hotel Spa \& Golf Resort, The Diamond Coast Hotel, Connemara Coast Hotel and Harvey’s Point Lakeside Resort.*** **About us** Windward are a team of highly experienced hotel operators and investors with a hotel portfolio of approx. 2,000 keys\* in Ireland and Continental Europe. Founded in 2007, Windward is a leading hospitality management company, managing and operating hotels on behalf of their owners. **Requirements** * 3rd level degree in Hotel or Hospitality Management or related field of study * Willingness to relocate to location across Ireland * Passionate about hotels and the hospitality industry Applications are now open so apply today! **About The Role** **The Programme** Commencing in September 2026, the new Windward Graduate Management Programme is an 18\-month, rotational programme designed to equip graduates with the skills, knowledge and experience necessary to become future leaders in the hospitality industry by providing comprehensive training and hands\-on experience across various departments within our hotels. The programme is structured into six 3\-month rotations across different departments across: * Front Office * Reservations \& Revenue Management * Food \& Beverage * Housekeeping * Support Functions (Finance, HR, Sales \& Marketing) * Ops Management This rotational approach allows graduates to gain in\-depth experience and an understanding of each department's operations and how they interconnect to deliver exceptional guest experiences. In addition to the day\-to\-day experience, Graduate Managers will be enrolled on numerous additional qualifications to enhance their professional and personal development including Management Training Level 5, Train the Trainer and much more. With guidance from General Managers and HR, the programme will be underpinned by a curriculum of learning objectives that the graduate must achieve to ensure they are on the right track. Each graduate will also be assigned a mentor within the hotel to provide support and guidance. The overall objective of the programme is to create a pipeline to secure and train excellent future managers and leaders across the group. **Required Criteria** **Skills Needed** **About The Company** Since its inception in 2007, Windward Management Limited has built a reputation for excellence in hotel management. With headquarters in Dublin, the company provides tailored solutions for hotel owners, including operations, marketing, and financial management. Windward's team is driven by a passion for delivering exceptional guest experiences while ensuring each property achieves its full potential. Recognized for its dedication to innovation and operational success, the company is equally committed to investing in its people. Professionals joining Windward Management will become part of a supportive and dynamic environment, with abundant opportunities to grow and excel within a thriving sector. **Company Culture** At Windward Management, people are at the heart of success. The company nurtures a culture rooted in respect, collaboration, and a shared commitment to excellence. Employees are empowered to innovate and contribute to meaningful outcomes while enjoying a strong focus on work\-life balance. Windward fosters professional growth through tailored development opportunities, ensuring each team member can achieve their career aspirations. This positive, inclusive workplace values individual contributions, encourages open communication, and celebrates success collectively. For hospitality professionals seeking a rewarding career in an environment where their efforts truly matter, Windward Management offers an exceptional opportunity to thrive. **Company Benefits** Windward Management Limited understands the value of its people and offers a benefits package designed to support and reward its employees. Competitive salaries are complemented by a supportive work environment that prioritizes work\-life balance and career development. Team members benefit from opportunities to learn, grow, and advance in their careers, with access to mentoring and professional training programs. The company places a strong emphasis on employee well\-being, fostering a culture of mutual respect and collaboration. For candidates looking for a career that combines growth, fulfillment, and an opportunity to make an impact, Windward Management provides an ideal platform. **Salary** Not disclosed
